name: District #6
description_short: District #6 Federal Credit Union is a nonprofit, member-owned credit union in Hornell, NY offering competitive rates on savings, loans, and banking services with personalized service.
description_long:
District #6 Federal Credit Union operates as a nonprofit financial institution headquartered in Hornell, New York, serving members in the local community. As a credit union, it is owned and controlled by its members rather than external shareholders, operating on a "people helping people" cooperative model. The institution has been established as a community financial resource with a focus on accessibility and member service.

The credit union offers a comprehensive range of financial services comparable to traditional banks, including checking and savings accounts, auto loans, personal loans, mobile deposit capabilities, online banking, and telephone banking options. Current promotional offerings include auto loan specials and personal loans up to $3,000 at 3.00% APR. Members can access curbside assistance for seniors and members with disabilities, and the institution provides multiple contact channels including phone, email, and in-person visits at their single Broadway location.

District #6 distinguishes itself through member-focused service, competitive pricing explicitly stated to be cheaper than traditional banks, and accessibility accommodations like curbside banking for elderly and disabled members. The organization emphasizes personal service with named staff contacts and flexible banking hours (7:30am-4:00pm weekdays). The nonprofit structure means profits are returned to members through better rates rather than distributed to external investors.

As a small, single-location credit union, District #6 has inherent limitations in technology and service breadth compared to larger financial institutions. Members must qualify for membership based on community or employer affiliation. The limited hours and single physical location may be inconvenient for members requiring extended availability or multiple branch access. However, for local Hornell-area residents seeking relationship-based banking with competitive rates, the credit union represents a viable community banking alternative.
services: Auto loans with current spring promotional special
Personal loans up to $3,000 at promotional 3.00% APR rate
Card activation and PIN change service
Curbside banking for seniors and members with disabilities
Debit card services with specialized activation line
Membership accounts with member-focused rates and service
Basic financial services comparable to traditional banks
pros: Nonprofit structure means rates and fees are competitive and member-favorable rather than profit-maximized
Personal loan special offering up to $3,000 at 3.00% APR is competitive for short-term borrowing
Curbside banking service specifically for seniors and members with disabilities increases accessibility
Mobile deposit capability allows members to deposit checks remotely via smartphone
Multiple contact options including phone banking (866-865-7892) and online banking reduce friction
Named staff members listed with direct email contacts provides personalized service relationship
Extended weekday morning hours (7:30am start) accommodate working members before traditional bank hours
cons: Single physical location in Hornell, NY limits convenience for members outside immediate area or requiring branch visits
Limited hours (7:30am-4:00pm weekdays only) with no weekend service excludes members needing off-hours access
Membership likely restricted to residents or employees in specific geographic area or employers, limiting accessibility
No drive-through despite being a full-service credit union suggests limited infrastructure compared to larger competitors
Small institution size may mean limited product variety, technology sophistication, or customer service resources
best_for: Hornell, NY area residents seeking relationship-based community banking with competitive rates
Seniors and members with disabilities who benefit from personalized curbside service options
Local employees or community members qualifying for membership seeking personal or auto loans
Members prioritizing nonprofit cooperative banking model over traditional for-profit bank structure
diagnosis: District #6 Federal Credit Union is best for residents of Hornell, NY and surrounding areas who value member-owned cooperative banking, competitive rates, and personalized service over large-bank convenience. The primary caveat is that membership appears restricted by geography or employer affiliation, and the single location with limited hours makes it impractical for members needing extensive branch access or off-hours banking support.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: District #6 — Credit Union in Hornell, NY | CreditDoc
meta_description: District #6 — NCUA-insured credit union in Hornell, NY. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
